title: Inbox Triage and Reply Drafts for Real Estate
metaDescription: Done-for-you inbox triage and reply drafts for real estate agents and property managers. Sorted email, drafts in your voice, faster responses, fewer lost deals.
excerpt: Your real estate inbox gets triaged and replies drafted in your voice, so buyer questions, owner requests, and showing inquiries get answered before they go cold.

sections.intro:
If you sell or manage property, your inbox is the deal. Buyer questions, tenant complaints, owner updates, lender requests, title chases, vendor invoices, and cold prospecting all land in the same place. The fast replies win listings and showings. The slow ones lose them. Most agents and property managers spend the first two hours of every day just figuring out what to answer and in what order, then writing the same five replies they wrote yesterday.

Inbox Triage and Reply Drafts is the Capabilities layer of your AI Operating System that does that first pass for you. It reads incoming email, sorts it by what actually matters in real estate (new buyer lead, showing request, offer, tenant issue, owner question, transaction task, vendor admin), and writes a draft reply in your voice. You still hit send. The system just removes the cold start.
sections.fourCFit:
This page sits in the Capabilities layer of the AIOS. Capabilities are the done-for-you workflows that draft, route, summarize, and follow up. To work well, this capability leans on the other three layers:

  • Context: your offers, your service areas, your listings in progress, your management portfolio, and your voice, so drafts sound like you and not a chatbot.
  • Connections: your email, calendar, CRM, and document storage, so drafts can quote the right showing time, the right property, and the right tenant or owner.
  • Cadence: a triage pass runs on a schedule through the day, so nothing sits unread for hours while you are at a showing or a closing.

It pairs naturally with the free 60-Second Lead Engine, which answers brand new leads in under 60 seconds. Triage handles everything that comes after the first reply.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
When this capability is installed and running, here is what you actually get:

  • Every incoming email sorted into clear real estate buckets: new buyer or seller lead, showing request, active transaction, tenant issue, owner request, vendor or admin, newsletter or noise.
  • A draft reply waiting in your drafts folder for anything that needs a human answer, written in your voice, using the property address, price, showing window, or tenant context already on file.
  • Urgent items (offer received, lease expiring, maintenance emergency, lender deadline) flagged at the top of the inbox with a one line summary.
  • Routine items (receipts, calendar confirmations, mass mailings) archived or filed without you touching them.
  • A short morning brief: how many emails came in overnight, how many drafts are ready, what needs your decision today.
  • SLA: drafts ready within 15 minutes of the email arriving during business hours, and within the next scheduled pass otherwise.
sections.howWeInstall:
We install this in three phases. You stay in your normal workflow the whole time.

  1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes): we look at a sample of your inbox, your current categories, and the replies you send most often. We confirm this capability is the right starting point, or point you somewhere better. Run the audit.
  2. Install (10 business days): we capture your voice from past sent mail, build the real estate categories that match how you actually work, connect your email and calendar, and stand up the triage and drafting workflow. You review drafts for the first week and we tune from your edits.
  3. Operate (ongoing): the system runs on its schedule. We add one new automation per week, for example offer summaries, showing follow ups, or owner monthly recaps, so the AIOS keeps getting more useful without getting more complicated. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
• First reply time on buyer and seller inquiries drops from hours to minutes during business hours.
  • Email handling time falls by 50 to 70 percent for most agents and property managers once drafts are tuned to your voice.
  • Roughly 8 to 15 hours a week back for the principal, depending on inbox volume and team size.
  • Fewer dropped threads on active transactions, because urgent items get surfaced instead of buried.
  • Standing guarantee: you recover 40 or more hours a month across the AIOS, or we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
Will the drafts actually sound like me, or like generic AI?

We train the drafting on your past sent mail and the way you talk to clients, owners, and tenants. The first week you edit drafts and we tune from those edits. By week two most agents send drafts with small tweaks or no edits at all.

Does this send replies automatically?

No. Drafts wait for you to send. You own the system and you own every reply that goes out. The only thing that runs without your click is the sorting, archiving, and the morning brief.

Can it handle both sales and property management in one inbox?

Yes. We build separate categories and draft styles for buyer leads, seller leads, active transactions, tenant issues, owner communications, and vendor admin. The same inbox, sorted by the work it actually represents.
